item.title: Delta suspends special treatment for Congress as shutdown sows chaos in airports
item.description: Delta Air Lines cited the impact ​of a partial government shutdown that has disrupted U.S. air travel.

item.title: Microsoft to rent Texas data center dropped by Oracle and OpenAI, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Microsoft has agreed to rent a data center project in Texas that ​was originally being developed for Oracle ‌and OpenAI, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the situation.

item.title: OpenAI non-profit names leaders, plans to spend $1 billion this year, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: OpenAI is ​making several ‌key hires to ​lead ​its non-profit arm ⁠and ​is ​planning to spend $1 billion ​in ​various AI-related investments ‌this ⁠year through the non-profit ​unit, ​Bloomberg ⁠News reported ​on ​Tuesday.

item.title: CERAWEEK US will roll out pilot surveys to track energy use by data centers
item.description: The U.S. Department of Energy's information arm will launch a series of pilot surveys on Wednesday aimed at ​gauging the energy use of the country's data centers, the ‌administrator of the agency told Reuters.

item.title: Poland to start work on a digital services tax, deputy PM says
item.description: Poland's government will start work on a digital services tax bill, Deputy Prime Minister and Digitalisation Minister Krzysztof Gawkowski ​said on Tuesday, setting up a potential clash with ‌key ally the United States.

item.title: Iran war starts to hit global economy, business surveys show
item.description: The Iran war is already taking a toll on major economies around the world, according to business surveys on Tuesday which showed how a ​surge in energy prices and rising uncertainty were dampening activity and pushing inflation expectations higher.

item.title: Microsoft president says winning trust of US communities is paramount to building data centers
item.description: Gaining the ​approval of local ‌communities has become paramount to building ​data centers ​in the U.S., Microsoft ⁠President Brad ​Smith said on ​Tuesday, as towns across the country increasingly protest ​the developments.

item.title: Traders bet $500 million on oil price just before Trump's post on delay to Iran attack
item.description: Traders bet half a billion dollars ​on the price of crude only 15 minutes before U.S. President Donald Trump announced a five-day delay to attacks ‌on Iran's energy infrastructure that sent the market plunging, exchange data and Reuters calculations showed.

item.title: US-Ukrainian joint investment project to invest in dual-use military tech firm
item.description: A joint U.S.-Ukrainian reconstruction fund will make its first investment in ​Sine Engineering, a Ukrainian dual-use military ‌technology company, a source familiar with the plans said on Tuesday, with further projects ​still under review.

item.title: Britain's FCA defends Palantir contract award before lawmakers
item.description: U.S. data analytics firm Palantir, which won a contract to supply AI systems ​to Britain's Financial Conduct Authority, will not have access to regulatory intelligence, an FCA official ‌said on Tuesday, as the deal came under criticism from lawmakers.

item.title: Apple to bring paid ads to maps to US, Canada this summer
item.description: Apple on Tuesday said it plans to introduce paid ads to ‌its maps service in the U.S. and Canada this summer, marking its entry into a business long dominated by Alphabet's Google.

item.title: Schindler ready to oppose potential Kone-TK Elevator merger, CEO says
item.description: Schindler would ​be ready to ‌challenge a merger deal between rivals ​Kone and TK ​Elevator before antitrust authorities, ⁠the head ​of the Swiss lift ​maker told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: Epic Games to cut more than 1,000 jobs as Fortnite usage falls
item.description: Epic Games said on Tuesday ​it would lay ‌off more than 1,000 employees as it ​cuts costs after ​a sharp fall in ⁠usage of its ​flagship game, Fortnite.

item.title: US business activity slips to 11-month low in March amid Iran war, S&P Global survey shows
item.description: U.S. business activity slowed to an 11-month low in March as the war in the Middle ​East raised prices for energy products and other inputs, a survey showed on Tuesday, reinforcing fears of an ‌acceleration in inflation in the months ahead.

item.title: NYSE teams up with Securitize to develop tokenized securities platform
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange, part of Intercontinental Exchange , announced a collaboration on Tuesday with digital asset ​company Securitize to help create tokenized versions of ‌traditional financial securities.

item.title: Dollar General names retail veteran Jerry Fleeman Jr. as new CEO
item.description: Dollar General on Tuesday named ​Jerry Fleeman Jr. as ‌its new chief executive officer, effective January 1, 2027, replacing Todd ​Vasos.

item.title: Zoox to widen US robotaxi footprint with San Francisco, Vegas expansion
item.description: Zoox, Amazon's self-driving unit, said on Tuesday it will expand its robotaxi service in ​San Francisco and Las Vegas, and begin ‌testing its purpose-built robotaxis in Austin and Miami, marking its broadest push yet into the U.S. autonomous ride-hailing ​market.

item.title: US fourth-quarter productivity growth revised sharply lower
item.description: U.S. worker productivity growth slowed more than initially thought in the fourth quarter, government data showed, ​boosting growth in unit labor costs.

item.title: FedEx launches same-day delivery service amid US delivery race
item.description: FedEx said on Tuesday it had launched a same-day delivery service ​with last-mile delivery software company OneRail, ‌as retailers and logistics firms race to speed up order fulfillment.

item.title: Russia launches low-orbit satellites in bid to one day rival Starlink
item.description: Russia said on Tuesday it had launched 16 low-orbit satellites as Moscow seeks ​to build a rival to Elon Musk's ‌Starlink network.

item.title: Peltz's Trian, General Catalyst step up Janus bid as takeover fight intensifies
item.description: Janus Henderson said on Tuesday that Trian and General Catalyst have raised their bid ​to buy the $493-billion asset manager to $52 per share, as it fends off a persistent ‌takeover pursuit by Victory Capital .

item.title: US Easter spending to touch a record $24.9 billion in 2026, NRF forecasts
item.description: U.S. consumer spending on Easter is expected to rise to a record $24.9 ​billion in 2026, a 5.5% increase ‌from a year earlier, industry body National Retail Federation said on Tuesday, signaling robust demand ​amid economic uncertainty.

item.title: Norway wealth fund moves towards some AI-driven decisions but with humans in control
item.description: Norway's $2.1 trillion sovereign wealth fund, the world's ​largest, will eventually allow some investment decisions to be made by AI systems under human supervision, ‌but not yet, as the tools still make errors, fund officials said on Tuesday.

item.title: BMO to launch tokenized cash platform with CME and Google Cloud
item.description: Bank of Montreal said on Tuesday it plans to roll out tokenized cash capabilities in collaboration with CME Group and ​Google Cloud, as it seeks to meet rising demand ‌for real‑time payments amid a global shift toward around‑the‑clock trading.

item.title: Barclays raises 2026 year-end S&P 500 target to 7,650 despite Middle East, inflation risks
item.description: Barclays raised its 2026 year-end S&P 500 target on Tuesday, betting that strong corporate earnings led by ​the technology sector and resilient economic growth will outweigh rising ‌macro risks, including war in the Middle East, AI-driven disruption and stress emerging in private credit markets.

item.title: United Airlines bets bigger on premium travel as Iran war drives up fuel costs
item.description: United Airlines on Tuesday ​unveiled a broad aircraft and cabin overhaul centered on higher-end seating, pressing ahead with ‌its long-term growth plan days after warning that oil could stay above $100 a barrel through 2027 and outlining plans to trim some flights in response.

item.title: Aspex wants Delivery Hero CEO out as investor's campaign heats up
item.description: Aspex, a top Delivery Hero shareholder, on Tuesday urged the online takeaway ​food group's supervisory board to oust ‌the CEO and his top management team in an escalation of the investor's campaign for a ​change in strategic direction.

item.title: Morning Bid: From 48 hours to five days
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: US stock futures dip as Middle East uncertainty tempers relief rally
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were down on ​Tuesday, after a relief rally in the previous session, as renewed doubts over easing Middle East ‌tensions weighed on sentiment despite President Donald Trump's decision to delay strikes on Iran's power grid.

item.title: Smithfield Foods beat quarterly results estimates on resilient demand for pork
item.description: Smithfield Foods beat analysts' estimates for fourth-quarter sales and profit on Tuesday, helped by resilient demand for packaged meats ​and cost-saving efforts, lifting the U.S. pork processor's shares ‌nearly 4% higher in premarket trading.
